evidence: line reads '合理利用 cache（actions/cache）加速构建' with no cache-key strategy specified at this point
description: Vague quantifier '合理利用' (use cache reasonably) without an operational definition
suggested_fix: Specify the cache-key strategy inline (e.g. hash of lockfile) or point to the later '缓存策略' note

evidence: checklist item '设置适当的权限（permissions）' gives no minimal-permission set
description: Vague quantifier '适当的权限' (appropriate permissions) without an operational definition
suggested_fix: Name the minimal permission set to default to, e.g. 'contents: read unless a job needs to write'

evidence: checklist item '超时时间合理（timeout-minutes）' gives no default or range
description: Vague quantifier '合理' (reasonable timeout) without a default or range
suggested_fix: Give a concrete default range, e.g. '10-20 minutes for standard CI jobs'

evidence: criterion '是否合理使用' design patterns is listed with no evaluation criteria
description: Vague quantifier '合理使用' (used reasonably) without stated criteria
suggested_fix: List concrete anti-pattern signals (e.g. singleton without thread-safety, factory with a single concrete type) instead of 'reasonable'

evidence: note '也要检查是否恰当' for Chinese identifiers/comments gives no criteria
description: Vague quantifier '恰当' (appropriate) without stated criteria for Chinese identifier/comment review
suggested_fix: State the concrete check, e.g. 'flag Chinese identifiers that mix pinyin and English inconsistently'

evidence: principle 7 reads '尽量补上社交证明信号' with no minimum bar
description: Vague quantifier '尽量' (try to) without a minimum bar for social-proof signals
suggested_fix: State when social proof should be included, e.g. 'include Star count whenever gh repo view succeeds'

evidence: bullet reads '尽量收敛到 1 个正式入口' — the '尽量' qualifier is undefined even though the target count is given
description: Vague quantifier '尽量' (try to) softening an otherwise concrete '1 个入口' target
suggested_fix: Drop '尽量' and state the rule as a hard requirement, or state the exception conditions explicitly

evidence: commit message template placeholder reads '<必要时说明原因、关键实现和兼容性>' with no trigger condition for '必要时'
description: Vague quantifier '必要时' (when necessary) without an explicit trigger condition
suggested_fix: State the trigger, e.g. 'when the diff touches a public API or changes behavior'

evidence: note reads '每次重构尽量小步提交，便于回滚' with no size/step definition
description: Vague quantifier '尽量小步' (try to take small steps) without an operational size definition
suggested_fix: Define 'small step' concretely, e.g. 'one refactor pattern per commit, each independently revertable'

evidence: "playwright": "^1.40.0" uses a caret range, not an exact pin
description: playwright dependency is unpinned (caret range), allowing automatic minor/patch upgrades
suggested_fix: Pin to an exact version and commit a lockfile for the scripts/ workspace

description: Sends the Juejin session Cookie to api.juejin.cn to create an article draft
suggested_fix: No fix needed if intended first-party use; document the outbound Cookie flow in the script header

description: Builds a Node.js script via unescaped f-string interpolation of an API-derived draft_id (embedded at line 84), writes it to a temp file, and executes it with subprocess.run(['node', tmp_file]); a malformed draft_id could break out of the embedded string literal
suggested_fix: Validate draft_id matches ^\d+$ before interpolation, or pass it via process.env/argv instead of string interpolation

evidence: tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.js', delete=False) with no dir= argument writes to the OS temp directory
description: Generated JS script is written to the OS temp directory outside the repo tree and not explicitly cleaned up on all paths
suggested_fix: Write to a dedicated gitignored .tmp/ directory under the repo, or ensure unlink runs even on early exit

description: Sends the Juejin session Cookie to api.juejin.cn to create an article draft
suggested_fix: No fix needed if intended first-party use; document the outbound Cookie flow in the script header

description: Error/result screenshots and juejin_result.json are written to the OS temp directory, outside the repo tree
suggested_fix: Write to a dedicated gitignored .tmp/ directory under the repo instead of os.tmpdir()

evidence: https.request({hostname: 'api.juejin.cn', ..., headers: {'Cookie': COOKIE, ...}}) inside a script that requires --publish to run
description: Live-smoke script sends the Juejin session Cookie to api.juejin.cn and, with --publish, creates and publishes a real article on the maintainer's account
suggested_fix: No fix needed given the explicit --publish guard; consider adding a --dry-run-only default alias for CI

description: Draft/publish screenshots are written to the OS temp directory, outside the repo tree
suggested_fix: Write to a dedicated gitignored .tmp/ directory under the repo instead of os.tmpdir()

description: Invokes gh CLI to perform an external GraphQL network call fetching GitHub star counts for every curated repo URL in README.md
suggested_fix: No fix needed; this is a read-only, non-credentialed (uses gh's own auth) query intrinsic to the script's stated purpose

evidence: all 20 skills/*/SKILL.md directories appear in README.md's skills table with matching relative links; scripts/test_sync_readme_to_site.py::test_original_skill_sets_and_promo_counts_are_consistent already asserts this at test time
description: No broken references or orphaned skill directories found; README, docs/index.html, and PROMO.md skill counts are cross-checked by an existing repo test
